name: BLAST On Slurm
summary: Run local BLAST+ searches on Slurm with bounded smoke-test defaults.
description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm pattern for local BLAST+ searches, including a tiny FASTA smoke test, local database creation with makeblastdb, explicit CPU and output bounds, and dry-run-first launch records.

inputs.name: query_fasta
inputs.description: FASTA query file for blastn, blastp, or another BLAST+ search program.

inputs.name: reference_fasta
inputs.description: FASTA file used to build a local BLAST database for the smoke test or production search.

inputs.name: blast_program
inputs.description: BLAST+ search executable such as blastn or blastp.

inputs.name: output_dir
inputs.description: Directory for BLAST result files, launch records, and generated database files.

outputs.name: blast_launch_plan
outputs.description: Reviewable command, input paths, database path, CPU count, result path, and output controls.

outputs.name: blast_database
outputs.description: Local makeblastdb output files under the record directory or configured database directory.

outputs.name: blast_results
outputs.description: Tabular BLAST result file when RUN_BLAST=1.

examples.title: Plan or run a local BLAST+ smoke test on Slurm

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check the Slurm wrapper shell syntax.
Submit the wrapper in default dry-run mode, then set RUN_BLAST=1 on a short test allocation after confirming the BLAST+ module, query, reference, and output paths.

references.title: BLAST Command Line Applications User Manual
